summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on three cases: BZA 2022-011 (an appeal regarding an illegal six-foot privacy fence in a front yard at 6823 Goshen Road), BZA 2022-012 (an appeal of a stop work order on an unauthorized commercial truck repair business operating in an R3 residential zone at the same address), and BZA 2022-023 (an area variance request for a panhandle/flag lot split at 6790 Gainer Road to allow a family member to build a house). The board denied both appeals for the Kelly property cases and began hearing the lot split variance case.
decisions: Motion to approve previous meeting minutes passed (Curie: Yes, Seagla: Yes, Schall: Abstain, Theodore: Yes)
Motion to deny appeal for BZA 2022-011 (fence at 6823 Goshen Road) passed unanimously (Curie: Yes, Siegler: Yes, Shaw: Yes, Theodore: Yes)
Motion to deny appeal for BZA 2022-012 (stop work order on business at 6823 Goshen Road) passed unanimously (Curie: Yes, Seagla: Yes, Shaw: Yes, Theodore: Yes)
action_items: BZA to continue hearing and deliberate on BZA 2022-023 (Seabolt/Edwards lot split variance at 6790 Gainer Road)
Jeffrey Kelly's attorney indicated intent to appeal adverse decisions to the Court of Common Pleas
key_amounts: $229 per year in taxes on Kelly property before business operation
$3,500 per year in taxes after business classification change
$4,300 in taxes for 2022 on Kelly property
14,000 square feet of parking area at Kelly business
60 by 80 foot building (plus 12 foot lean-to) at Kelly property
70 acres total for Seabolt property at 6790 Gainer Road
6-acre, 16-acre, and 48-acre proposed lot splits at Gainer Road
150-foot minimum lot width required in R1 zoning
28-foot setback of Kelly building from property line (100 feet required)
75-foot setback required to residential district for commercial development
notable_topics: Appeal of fence denial - six-foot privacy fence installed without permit in front yard at 6823 Goshen Road
Appeal of stop work order on unauthorized truck repair business operating in R3 residential zone
Dispute over prior zoning director Bob Seifried's interpretation and approval of agricultural exemption for commercial use
Whether a new zoning director can reverse a prior director's interpretation and enforcement decisions
Zone change from R3 to M2 previously denied by Clermont County Planning Commission, Goshen Township Zoning Commission, and Board of Trustees
Area variance request for panhandle/flag lot split at 6790 Gainer Road
Stacking of panhandle lots and road frontage requirements in R1 zoning
Difference between county auditor property classification and township zoning classification
attendees: Mr. Holmes (BZA member - absent at roll call)
Harry Holbert (Township Zoning Staff/Planning and Zoning Director)
Taylor Trout (attorney for Jeffrey Kelly, Keating and Musing and Klee Camp)
Jeff Preble (former Goshen Township property code officer)
Francis Kelly (6825 Goshen Road, support for applicant)
Mayor Tom Maffet (6853 Goshen Road, opposed)
Cassandra Edwards (applicant for BZA 2022-023, 1306 Sandwood Drive)
Victoria and Rodney Seabolt (property owners, 6790 Gainer Road)
Bob Seifried (former Goshen Township zoning director, referenced but not present)
notes: Transcript is truncated during the BZA 2022-023 hearing so the final outcome of that case is unknown. The Kelly cases had extensive history including prior continuances, a denied zone change (denied by Clermont County Planning Commission, Goshen Township Zoning Commission, and Board of Trustees), and a prior denied use variance. Former township employee Jeff Preble testified that prior zoning director Bob Seifried routinely exempted properties over 5 acres as agricultural regardless of actual use. Attorney Taylor Trout preserved client's right to appeal to Court of Common Pleas. Mr. Holmes appeared absent from roll call. The meeting opened at 6:32 PM.
